  langs/
    en/                      : Language file for \`en\` locale
      phrase.php             : Define phrases for group \`phrase\`
      validator.php          : Define phrases for group \`validator\`
  menu/                      :

  routes/                    :

      Migrations             : Contain database [migrations](https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/migrations#main-content)
      Seeders                : Contain database [seeders](https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/seeding#introduction)
        PackageSeeder.php    : Entry point for database seeder

    <h2 {...{

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{

      "providers": ["MetaFox\\\\Blog\\\\Providers\\\\BlogServiceProvider"],

`}</code></pre>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`name`}</inlineCode>{`: The name of the app package. It consists of vendor name and project name, separated by a slash (/) `}</p>
    <p>{`For examples:`}</p>

    <p>{`The app name MUST be `}<em parentName="p">{`lowercase`}</em>{` and consist of words separated by `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`-`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`_`}</inlineCode>{`. The complete name should match the regular expression `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`^[a-z0-9]([_.-]?[a-z0-9]+)_/[a-z0-9](<([_.]?|-{0,2})[a-z0-9]+>)\\_\\$`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`description`}</inlineCode>{`: A short description of the app package. Shoule be a one-line message.`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`version`}</inlineCode>{`: a string specifing the version of the app package. In most cases, this field is not required and can be omitted (see below).`}</p>

    </ul>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`authors`}</inlineCode>{`: Info of the authors of the package. You can specify multiple `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`author`}</inlineCode>{` objects here. An `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`author`}</inlineCode>{` object can have following properties:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`name`}</inlineCode>{`: The author's name. Usually their real name.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`email`}</inlineCode>{`: The author's email address.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`homepage`}</inlineCode>{`: URL to the author's website.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`require`}</inlineCode>{`: Map of packages required by this package. The app package will not be installed unless all requirements are met.`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`require-dev`}</inlineCode>{`: Map of packages required for developing this package, or running tests, etc. The dev requirements of the root package are installed by default. Both `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`install`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`update`}</inlineCode>{` commands support the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`--no-dev`}</inlineCode>{` option to prevent dev dependencies from being installed.`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`autoload.psr-4`}</inlineCode>{`: Under the psr-4 key you can define a mapping from namespaces to paths relative to the package root. When autoloading a class like `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Foo\\\\Bar\\\\Baz`}</inlineCode>{`, and a namespace prefixed `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Foo\\\\`}</inlineCode>{` are pointed to a directory `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`src/`}</inlineCode>{`, the autoloader will look for a file named `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`src/Bar/Baz.php`}</inlineCode>{` and include it if existing. Note that as opposed to the older PSR-0 style, the prefix `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`(Foo\\\\)`}</inlineCode>{` is not present in the file path.`}</p>
    <p>{`Namespace prefixes must end in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`\\\\`}</inlineCode>{` to avoid conflicts between similar prefixes. For example: Prefix `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Foo`}</inlineCode>{` would match classes in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`FooBar`}</inlineCode>{` namespace. Thus, we use the trailing backslashes to solve the problem: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Foo\\\\`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`FooBar\\\\`}</inlineCode>{` are distinct.`}</p>
    <p>{`The PSR-4 references are all combined, during installation or update, into a single key which may be found in the generated file `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`vendor/composer/autoload_psr4.php`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`autoload.files`}</inlineCode>{`: If you want to require certain files explicitly on every request then you can use the file autoloading mechanism. This is very useful if your app package needs to include PHP functions that cannot be autoloaded by PHP`}</p>

    <p><inlineCode parentName="p">{`autoload-dev`}</inlineCode>{`: This section allows to define autoload rules for development purposes.`}</p>

    <p>{`Therefore, it is a good solution to rely on a dedicated path for your unittests and add it within the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`autoload-dev`}</inlineCode>{` section.`}</p>
